headline: This Earnings Season Is the Stock Market’s Biggest Test
summary: Baseball legend Tommy Lasorda, the former Los Angeles Dodgers manager who won two World Series, didn’t have much time for the idea of “pressure” in professional sports.  Stock markets are different matter, however.  The looming second-quarter earnings season—set against the pullback in big tech stocks, the resurgence of global geopolitical risks, and the specter of renewed inflation—has investors on edge.

Stock markets are different matter, however.  The looming second-quarter earnings season—set against the pullback in big tech stocks, the resurgence of global geopolitical risks, and the specter of renewed inflation—has investors on edge.

headline: SK Hynix Stock Is Rising Ahead of Pricing for Oversubscribed U.S. Listing
summary: SK Hynix  shares rose on Thursday ahead of the South Korean company’s listing of American depositary receipts.  The memory giant is launching an ADR listing on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SKHY on Friday.  Ten ADRs will represent one local South Korean share, giving an implied dollar price per ADR of around $144.50.

content.title: SK Hynix Stock Is Rising Ahead of Pricing for Oversubscribed U.S. Listing
content.summary: SK Hynix  shares rose on Thursday ahead of the South Korean company’s listing of American depositary receipts.  The memory giant is launching an ADR listing on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SKHY on Friday.  Ten ADRs will represent one local South Korean share, giving an implied dollar price per ADR of around $144.50.

headline: Memory monthly sales hit record $74.6B as analysts see price surge ahead
summary: Investing.com - Global memory monthly sales reached a record $74.6 billion, surging 31.7% month-on-month and running 2.8 percentage points above the 10-year seasonal average, according to UBS's July Memory Monthly report — a data point that has both UBS and Bernstein forecasting sharp contract price increases ahead, even as the two firms diverge sharply on how long the rally can last.

content.title: Memory monthly sales hit record $74.6B as analysts see price surge ahead
content.summary: Investing.com - Global memory monthly sales reached a record $74.6 billion, surging 31.7% month-on-month and running 2.8 percentage points above the 10-year seasonal average, according to UBS's July Memory Monthly report — a data point that has both UBS and Bernstein forecasting sharp contract price increases ahead, even as the two firms diverge sharply on how long the rally can last.

headline: SK Hynix's US listing more than seven times oversubscribed, source says
summary: Demand for SK Hynix's $28 billion U.S. share sale was more than seven times available shares, a person familiar with the matter said, underscoring huge investor appetite for a pivotal company in the AI supply ‌chain.  SK Hynix declined to comment.  In addition to bringing in funds, SK Hynix's U.S. listing is expected to help it narrow its valuation gap with U.S. rival Micron, which, despite having less market share in key memory products, has benefited from direct access to the world's largest pool of investors.

content.title: SK Hynix's US listing more than seven times oversubscribed, source says
content.summary: Demand for SK Hynix's $28 billion U.S. share sale was more than seven times available shares, a person familiar with the matter said, underscoring huge investor appetite for a pivotal company in the AI supply ‌chain.  SK Hynix declined to comment.  In addition to bringing in funds, SK Hynix's U.S. listing is expected to help it narrow its valuation gap with U.S. rival Micron, which, despite having less market share in key memory products, has benefited from direct access to the world's largest pool of investors.

headline: Micron boosts U.S. investment plan again, commits $250 billion through 2035
summary: Micron Technology said on Thursday it plans to invest ‌more than $250 billion in the ‌U.S. through 2035, driven by surging demand for ​memory chips in the AI era and President Donald Trump's push to bolster domestic chip production.  The new investment plan ‌represents a jump ⁠from the $200 billion that Micron announced last June, which was ⁠already increased by $30 billion from its original spending plans.  As part of the investment, ​Micron said ​it would ​spend $3 billion on strengthening ‌the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, of which $500 million will be used to fund advancements in GlobalWafers' 300-mm raw silicon wafer manufacturing facility in Sherman, Texas.

content.title: Micron boosts U.S. investment plan again, commits $250 billion through 2035
content.summary: Micron Technology said on Thursday it plans to invest ‌more than $250 billion in the ‌U.S. through 2035, driven by surging demand for ​memory chips in the AI era and President Donald Trump's push to bolster domestic chip production.  The new investment plan ‌represents a jump ⁠from the $200 billion that Micron announced last June, which was ⁠already increased by $30 billion from its original spending plans.  As part of the investment, ​Micron said ​it would ​spend $3 billion on strengthening ‌the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, of which $500 million will be used to fund advancements in GlobalWafers' 300-mm raw silicon wafer manufacturing facility in Sherman, Texas.

headline: SK Hynix to price US offering at $149 per ADR, source says
summary: South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix intends to price its American Depository Receipts at $149 on Thursday to raise about $26.5 billion, a person familiar with the matter said.  The share sale decision comes as SK Hynix leverages its position as the leading supplier of high-bandwidth memory ‌chips, a critical component for the advanced processors powering global artificial intelligence systems.  Demand for SK Hynix's U.S. share sale was more than seven times available shares, another person familiar with the matter said, ​underscoring huge investor appetite for a pivotal company in the AI supply chain.

headline: Micron boosts US investment plan again, commits $250 billion through 2035
summary: Micron Technology said on Thursday it plans to invest more than $250 billion in the U.S. through 2035, driven by ‌surging demand for memory chips in the AI era and President ‌Donald Trump's push to bolster domestic chip production.  The new investment plan represents a jump from ​the $200 billion that Micron announced last June, which was already increased by $30 billion from its original spending plans.  Shares of Micron were up about 8% in early trading, adding to a more than 200% surge in value so far this year.

content.title: Micron boosts US investment plan again, commits $250 billion through 2035
content.summary: Micron Technology said on Thursday it plans to invest more than $250 billion in the U.S. through 2035, driven by ‌surging demand for memory chips in the AI era and President ‌Donald Trump's push to bolster domestic chip production.  The new investment plan represents a jump from ​the $200 billion that Micron announced last June, which was already increased by $30 billion from its original spending plans.  Shares of Micron were up about 8% in early trading, adding to a more than 200% surge in value so far this year.

headline: U.S. chip stocks rebound as Meta plans to double GW capacity, China semis rally
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. chip stocks bounced back Thursday, recovering some of the ground lost earlier in the week, after a rally in Chinese semiconductor shares helped steady investor nerves following a selloff following Samsung Electronics’ latest results. Additionally, fears of an AI CapEx slowdown eased after Reuters reported that Meta Platforms plans to double its computing power to 14 gigawatts next year and expects its custom AI chip to start production in September.

headline: Tech Stocks Led Today's Rally and the Rest of the Market Followed
summary: The broadening trade was in full effect on Thursday, but that doesn’t mean tech was left behind. The Nasdaq led gains, up 1.3%. The S&P 500 rose 0.8%. The Dow inched up 129 points or 0.3%. But unlike the narrow leadership that dominated previous AI bull market rallies, Big Tech wasn’t the only one pulling its weight.

headline: U.S. chip stocks rebound as Meta plans to double GW capacity
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. chip stocks bounced back Thursday, recovering some of the ground lost earlier in the week, after a rally in Chinese semiconductor shares helped steady investor nerves following a selloff following Samsung Electronics’ latest results. Additionally, fears of an AI CapEx slowdown eased after Reuters reported that Meta Platforms plans to double its computing power to 14 gigawatts next year and expects its custom AI chip to start production in September.

content.title: U.S. chip stocks rebound as Meta plans to double GW capacity
content.summary: Investing.com -- U.S. chip stocks bounced back Thursday, recovering some of the ground lost earlier in the week, after a rally in Chinese semiconductor shares helped steady investor nerves following a selloff following Samsung Electronics’ latest results. Additionally, fears of an AI CapEx slowdown eased after Reuters reported that Meta Platforms plans to double its computing power to 14 gigawatts next year and expects its custom AI chip to start production in September.

headline: US stocks end higher; chip surge offsets Iran worries
summary:
STORY: U.S. stocks closed higher on Thursday, with the Dow adding more than a quarter of a percent, the S&P 500 climbing more than eight-tenths of a percent and the tech-heavy Nasdaq gaining 1.3%

:: Micron Technology

Chip stocks rallied, sparked by a 4.5% jump in shares of Micron Technology. The memory chip maker laid out plans to invest more than $250 billion in domestic chip manufacturing through 2035 to boost supply amid the AI boom.

Micron's peers also joined the rally, with Applied Materials climbing more than 3% and SanDisk surging over 7.5%.

AI-related stocks have been volatile lately as investors worried about the sustainability of a rally that has helped Wall Street reach record levels in 2026.

Gina Martin Adams is chief market strategist at HB Wealth.

"Tech has obviously been in the limelight of equity markets for a while, but that worked against stocks in the month of June. We'll see if the month of July brings something different. Obviously, the sector is dealing with a lot of issues, including the upcoming earnings season, where expectations are pretty high, as well as a tremendous amount of new shares coming to the market. First with SpaceX and now with SK Hynix out of South Korea."

Shares of Meta Platforms rose more than 4.5% after Reuters reported that the company plans to manufacture AI chips starting in September.

:: PepsiCo

Elsewhere in the market, shares of PepsiCo fell more than 3% despite the company beating second-quarter revenue estimates. PepsiCo warned of higher commodity costs in the second half of the year at a time when the snack and beverage giant is increasing investments and lowering prices to attract value-conscious consumers.

And shares of Costco sank more than 4% to a six-month low after the retailer reported decelerating comparable sales for June.

content.title: US stocks end higher; chip surge offsets Iran worries

headline: SK Hynix is the talk of the town. Is IPO day the right time to buy?
summary: SK Hynix (000660.KS) is set to go public in the US on Friday. Hennion & Walsh chief investment officer Kevin Mahn, Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré, and Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre take a closer look at the company, the memory trade, and more.
